message:
fix(security): reject null bytes in firewall intent and network guard validation

Prevent null byte injection attacks where strings like "evil.com\0.safe.com"
could be truncated to "evil.com" by downstream C-string resolvers, bypassing
host-based security policies.

intent_firewall.rs:
- Add null byte validation to RemoteEffect::validate() for target_host,
  path, and method fields
- Document the C-string truncation attack vector in code comments

network_guard.rs:
- Add null byte rejection in host_matches() with early return false
- Document bypass scenario where null bytes could defeat host matching

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
